dataset: one synthetic StrategyDecision + LearningDecision + NewsSignal triple, arbitrated 10,000 times per policy (SafetyFirstPolicy, ConsensusPolicy, WeightedVotePolicy, ConfidencePolicy)
source: tests/orchestration/test_performance.py's four scenarios, re-run standalone with resource.getrusage wrapped around each policy's arbitrate loop
known_gaps: All four policies measured against the same fixed triple (one learner disagreement direction, one positive news signal) -- per-policy latency does not vary meaningfully with agreement/disagreement outcome since none perform any I/O or heavier computation on either path.

memory_mb (148.98) is consistent with backtest/risk/strategy's own historical ~148-156mb, not evidence orchestration itself has a large footprint -- orchestration imports strategy.models (for StrategyDecision), which transitively imports hmm/features' pandas/numpy/scipy dependency chain via strategy.interfaces' RegimeState/FeatureVector type references, the same whole-decision-pipeline import graph every downstream package in this repo pays for. memory/nlp's own benchmarks (~30-35mb) do not import strategy and so don't pay this cost.

Does not benchmark orchestration.evaluate/generate_evaluation_report -- both are simple list comprehensions over already-computed FinalDecisions, no heavier than the metrics already benchmarked in Milestones 8-10's own evaluation modules.
